qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: ECDSA Signature Validation Bypass via Malformed DER Structure
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and code: this finding concerns ECDSA DER parsing in src/ecdsa-modified-1.0.js, not ext/jsbn2.js bnModInverse/BigInteger.modInverse handling of zero or negative inputs.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: ECDSA Signature Malleability via Missing Low-s Requirement
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and code: this finding concerns ECDSA low-s canonicalization in src/ecdsa-modified-1.0.js, not ext/jsbn2.js bnModInverse/BigInteger.modInverse infinite looping on zero or negative inputs.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory is a jsrsasign denial of service in ext/jsbn2.js bnModInverse/BigInteger.modInverse for zero or negative inputs, while the manifest-listed qualifying findings cover ECDSA DER parsing and ECDSA low-s malleability.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-8g7p-jf3g-gxcp describes CWE-835 infinite loop in bnModInverse/BigInteger.modInverse in ext/jsbn2.js for modInverse(0, m) or modInverse(-1, m).
ecdsa_der_bypass reports parseSig()/verify() behavior in src/ecdsa-modified-1.0.js with malformed DER trailing bytes.
ecdsa_malleability reports verifyRaw() accepting both (r, s) and (r, n-s) signatures in src/ecdsa-modified-1.0.js.
